title: Carnivore Macros Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate your carnivore diet macros: 30% protein, 70% fat. Daily meal breakdown for beef, fish, eggs & butter. Zero-carb nutrition guide.
intro: The **carnivore diet** is the most extreme form of low-carb eating: only animal-based foods, zero vegetables, zero carbohydrates. Macro breakdown: **30% protein / 70% fat / 0% carbs** from beef, fish, eggs, butter, and bone marrow. Popularized by Shawn Baker and Jordan Peterson, it lacks long-term clinical trials but has anecdotal reports of improvements in autoimmune diseases, depression, and severe obesity. Harvard published an observational study in 2021 (Lennerz et al.) with 2,029 participants. This calculator gives you daily grams based on your calorie target, but carnivore is an extreme elimination diet: it should be followed short-term (30–90 days) and under medical supervision, with bloodwork before and after (lipid panel, kidney and liver function).
keyTakeaway: Carnivore: **30% protein + 70% fat + 0% carbs**. 2000 kcal = 150g protein, 156g fat. Animal foods only. Medical supervision required.

example.title: Example: 2000 calorie carnivore day
example.result: Typical day: 500g beef + 4 eggs + 50g butter + occasional liver. Water + salt + electrolytes.

explanation:
## What is the carnivore diet

Complete elimination of plant foods.

## Allowed foods

- Red meat, fatty fish, eggs, organ meats.
- Butter, ghee, tallow.
- Some options: whole-fat dairy, hard cheese.

## Diet variations

- **Lion diet**: beef only, salt, water.
- **Ketovore**: carnivore + some leafy greens.

## Reported benefits

- Remission of autoimmune conditions (not validated by RCTs).
- Rapid weight loss.
- Mental clarity.
- Zero cravings.

## Risks

- Fiber deficiency (impacts gut microbiota).
- Vitamin C deficiency.
- LDL cholesterol elevation.
- Not sustainable long-term for most people.

## Common mistakes

1. Eating only lean meat without fat: 'rabbit starvation'.
2. Not supplementing electrolytes.
3. No medical monitoring.

## Supplements & related tools

- [Hydration calculator](/hydration-heat-climate).
- [Electrolytes pre/post-workout](/electrolytes-pre-post-workout).
- [Keto macros calculator](/macros-keto-diet).

Consult your physician. Pre-diet bloodwork is mandatory.

faq.q: Is the carnivore diet safe long-term?
faq.a: **No long-term evidence exists yet.** Most studies are observational and short-term. Longer-term safety data is needed.

faq.q: Can you get scurvy on the carnivore diet?
faq.a: Unlikely if you eat fresh meat and liver, which contain vitamin C. But margins are narrow—monitor vitamin C status.

faq.q: How much weight will you lose on carnivore?
faq.a: **5–15 kg (11–33 lbs) in 3 months** is typical, but results vary significantly by individual.

faq.q: Can you exercise and train on the carnivore diet?
faq.a: Yes, but expect a 3–6 week adaptation period before athletic performance fully recovers.

faq.q: Does the carnivore diet raise cholesterol?
faq.a: LDL cholesterol typically rises. Work with your doctor to monitor your lipid panel regularly.

faq.q: What scientific evidence supports the carnivore diet?
faq.a: The Lennerz Harvard 2021 study (2,029 participants) is observational. No large randomized controlled trials exist yet.

faq.q: Do you need electrolytes on the carnivore diet?
faq.a: Yes. Many people report improved energy and symptoms with added salt and electrolyte supplementation.

faq.q: Is the lion diet the same as carnivore?
faq.a: No. Lion diet is stricter: beef, salt, water only. Carnivore allows fish, eggs, and dairy products.

sources.name: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
